title: Present Perfect vs Past Simple
description: Master when to use 'have done' versus 'did' — one of the most important distinctions in English grammar.

items.front: Present Perfect: unspecified time / relevance now
items.back: I've been to Rome. (at some point)
items.example: She has read that book. (no specific time = still relevant)

items.back: I went to Rome last year.
items.example: She read that book in 2020. (specific year = clearly finished)

items.front: PP signal words: ever, never, already, yet, just, recently
items.example: I've already seen this film. She hasn't called yet.

items.front: PS signal words: yesterday, ago, last week/year, in 2020, when I was…
items.back: I went yesterday. Three years ago…
items.example: I started this job three years ago. We met when we were students.

items.back: I've worked here for five years.
items.example: She has lived in Madrid since 2018. (still lives there)

items.back: I worked there for two years. (and left)
items.example: He lived in Japan for a year. (he no longer lives there)

items.front: Biographical questions: Have you ever vs Did you ever
items.back: Have you ever = at any point (still possible) / Did you ever = in a completed period
items.example: Have you ever visited Rome? / Did you ever visit Rome when you lived in Italy?

items.front: Today / this week / this year → PP in British English
items.back: I've been busy today. / She's called twice this morning.
items.example: I've eaten twice today. (the day isn't over yet)

activities.title: Choose: present perfect or past simple

items.prompt: ___ to Paris? — Yes, I went there in 2019. It was beautiful.
items.explanation: The question asks about life experience → present perfect: Have you been. The answer gives a specific past time (2019) → past simple: I went.

items.prompt: I ___ that film last Friday with my sister. We loved it.
items.explanation: 'Last Friday' is a specific past time → past simple: I saw.

items.prompt: She ___ for this company since January. She's doing really well.
items.explanation: 'Since January' with ongoing relevance → present perfect: has worked. She still works there.

items.prompt: I've read three books this week — and it's only Wednesday!
items.explanation: 'This week' is an incomplete time period (still Wednesday) → present perfect is correct.

items.prompt: He ___ in Berlin for two years and then moved to Vienna.
items.explanation: He no longer lives in Berlin (he moved away) → completed past state → past simple: lived.

activities.title: PP or PS? Complete the gaps

items.sentence: I ___ never ___ sushi, but I want to try it soon. (eat)
She ___ the project last Thursday. She was so relieved! (finish)
I ___ Spanish since I was a teenager. I still practise every day. (study)
We ___ this city two years ago and immediately fell in love with it. (visit)
___ you ___ the report yet? The boss needs it before the meeting. (send)
